word: G649 - ἀποστέλλω - apostéllō - ap-os-tel'-lo - Put in

sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἀποστέλλω

- Transliteration: Apostello

- Phonetic: ap-os-tel'-lo

- Definition:

1. to order (one) to go to a place appointed

2. to send away, dismiss

a. to allow one to depart, that he may be in a state of liberty

b. to order one to depart, send off

c. to drive away

- Origin: from G575 and G4724

- TDNT entry: 07:38,7

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G575 and G4724; set apart that is (by implication) to send out (properly on a mission) literally or figuratively: - put in send (away forth out) set [at liberty].
